task_goal: Extract narrative entities that are neither Event nor Occasion. Return only entity name and description first; schema grounding will be done later.
global_constraints: Do not invent entities that are not grounded in the text.
Keep names close to the surface form and original language of the text; do not translate without reason.
extraction_rules: Favor recall, but keep only entities with clear textual support.
Prioritize stable characters, stable locations, key objects, and high-value concepts such as organizations, families, marriages, professions, or social identities.
Names that appear only in an incidental list and have no local narrative function in the current passage may be skipped.
Do not prioritize everyday generic objects, pure time modifiers, descriptive location phrases, or temporary group labels such as book, door, day, evening, a room where..., the area around....
If an entity is unlikely to enter the key relation network or recur stably later in the narrative, prefer not to extract it.

examples.name: Alcoholics Anonymous
examples.description: An organization or meeting system that supports recovery from alcohol abuse.

examples.name: buried bottle of alcohol
examples.description: A bottle hidden under the lawn that may be retrieved later.
